title: Source-Derived Photometric Sky Feature Baseline
summary: Rebuild the strong step-4 feature recipe from the best public-score run and measure it with a simple balanced model panel instead of the original heavy ensemble.
feature_strategy: Build a broad photometric-sky representation: raw ugriz magnitudes; all pairwise broad color indices such as u-g, u-r, u-i, u-z, g-r, g-i, g-z, r-i, and r-z; band-profile features including spectral slope over ugriz, residual mean absolute value, residual std, second-difference mean/std/absolute mean, magnitude mean/std/range, min/max magnitude, and min/max band labels; redshift features including absolute value, square, log1p(abs), signed log1p(abs), quantile bins, and interactions with spectral_type and galaxy_population; sky features including alpha/delta sin/cos, 3D unit-vector coordinates, cartesian interactions, harmonic sin/cos terms for orders 2 through 8, coarse and fine sky bins, bin-center offsets, and local sky neighbor-density/radius features; galactic-coordinate features from alpha/delta with l/b sin/cos, abs latitude, galactic bins, and categorical interactions; categorical crosses between spectral_type, galaxy_population, sky bins, redshift bins, galactic bins, and magnitude-extrema labels; covariate-only frequency features for sky/redshift/galactic bins; and covariate-only percentile-rank features for u, g, r, i, z, and redshift. If star_classification.csv is available, optionally append it as an auxiliary supervised domain using only shared columns and competition-standardized locality weights, but report whether the best simple model used auxiliary rows or competition-only training. Do not include multi-variant experts, affine calibration, or class-wise blend-weight search in the main hypothesis.
baseline_model_panel: Use a simple, comparable balanced panel: one CatBoost multiclass model with auto_class_weights="Balanced" and GPU when available, one XGBoost multiclass model with fold-specific balanced sample weights and GPU when available, one LightGBM multiclass model with class_weight="balanced" and CUDA smoke-test fallback, and optionally one HistGradientBoostingClassifier as a CPU sanity baseline. Train one configuration per model family; do not use variant ensembles, stacking, class-wise blending, affine calibration, temperature scaling, or hyperparameter search.
model_panel_rationale: The source run showed that this feature representation can support very strong models, with the best single calibrated expert around 0.9676 OOF balanced accuracy and the final heavy ensemble reaching 0.968045. A one-model-per-family panel isolates whether the feature recipe itself is the useful part, while still revealing whether CatBoost, XGBoost, LightGBM, or a simpler histogram tree is the best base learner for later development.
validation_strategy: Use 5-fold StratifiedKFold on the competition train rows and score balanced accuracy. For any auxiliary SDSS17 rows, train them only inside each fold and evaluate/report the primary CV score only on competition validation rows. For train+test covariate-only features such as frequency, ranks, bins, and neighbor density, never use class labels, target-derived values, OOF predictions, or model predictions. Report per-fold balanced accuracy and the best single model's mean 5-fold CV score; the score attached to this hypothesis should be the best simple model's CV=5 result, not the source run's final blended ensemble score.
materialization_hint: Materialize the source-derived recipe as explicit feature blocks: create u_g, u_r, u_i, u_z, g_r, g_i, g_z, r_i, and r_z color differences; compute ugriz SED slope, linear-trend residual mean absolute value/std, second-difference mean/absolute mean/std, magnitude mean/std/range, band_min_mag, band_max_mag, mag_min_band, and mag_max_band; add spectral_type__band_min_band and galaxy_population__band_max_band crosses; convert alpha/delta to sin/cos plus sky_x, sky_y, sky_z, sky_xy, sky_xz, sky_yz, sky_x2_minus_y2, and alpha/delta harmonic sin/cos features for orders 2 through 8; build 24x18 and 48x36 alpha/delta sky bins, sky cell ids, 24x18 bin centers/offsets, haversine nearest-neighbor radii for neighbors 5/10/16 in arcminutes, log density features for those radii, and a density ratio 5/16; add frequency features for sky bins/cells; add spectral_type and galaxy_population crosses with coarse/fine sky alpha and cell bins plus spectral_type__galaxy_population__sky_delta_bin_18; add redshift_abs, redshift_sq, redshift_log_abs, redshift_signed_log_abs, 20-quantile redshift bins from train+test covariates, redshift-bin frequency, and spectral_type/galaxy_population redshift-bin crosses; convert alpha/delta to galactic_l/galactic_b using the standard equatorial-to-galactic rotation, then add galactic l/b sin/cos, abs latitude, 24x12 galactic bins/cells, galactic-bin frequencies, and spectral_type/galaxy_population galactic-cell crosses; add train+test percentile ranks for u, g, r, i, z, and redshift. Keep the model stage deliberately small: fit the simple panel independently, log each model's fold scores and runtime, pick the best single model by mean CV balanced accuracy for submission, and write the standard submission, OOF, and test prediction artifacts. Do not add multi-variant probability weighting, global class-wise blend search, affine logit calibration, stacking, temperature scaling, or hyperparameter search.
expected_signal: If the feature recipe is the main reason for the public-score improvement, at least one simple model, probably XGBoost or LightGBM, should approach the source run's strongest single-expert range near 0.9675 CV while using less code and less ensemble overfitting. Feature importance or split diagnostics should emphasize color/profile, redshift-bin, sky/galactic, rank, and neighbor-density blocks. If the simple models fall far below the source run, the improvement likely depends on the heavy ensemble/calibration rather than the feature set alone.
risk: This intentionally overlaps with an already strong executed solution because it is a source-derived hypothesis, not a new idea from scratch. The main risk is methodological optimism from transductive covariate-only train+test features and from auxiliary-domain training; those must be logged clearly. Another risk is runtime and code size: the feature builder is broad, so the materialized code should avoid adding extra model complexity on top of it. Do not treat the original 0.968045 blended OOF as the hypothesis score target; use the best simple model's 5-fold competition-row CV score from the new run.
rationale:
Feature family: source_derived_photometric_sky_features
Feature strategy: Build a broad photometric-sky representation with color indices, band-profile/extrema features, redshift transforms and bins, sky trigonometric/cartesian/harmonic/bin/density features, galactic-coordinate features, categorical crosses, covariate-only frequency/rank features, and optional locality-weighted SDSS17 auxiliary rows. The hypothesis is to test this feature representation with simple single-model baselines, not to reproduce the original heavy ensemble.
Baseline model panel: one balanced CatBoost, one balanced XGBoost, one balanced LightGBM, and optionally one HistGradientBoosting sanity baseline; one configuration per model family, no variant ensembles, stacking, class-wise blending, affine calibration, or hyperparameter search.
Model panel rationale: The source run's final 0.968045 OOF/public-strong result used a heavy blender, but its single calibrated XGBoost/LightGBM experts were already around 0.9675; this hypothesis isolates the reusable feature set as a foundation for future development.
Validation strategy: 5-fold StratifiedKFold on competition rows, primary balanced accuracy reported only on competition validation folds, with optional auxiliary rows included only as training-domain augmentation inside each fold. The score assigned to this hypothesis should be the best simple model's mean CV=5 result.
implementation_hint: Implement the concrete feature blocks listed in materialization_hint and route them through one add_all_features-style function that applies identical transformations to train and test, using train+test covariates only for unsupervised bins, frequencies, neighbor-density, and percentile-rank features. If star_classification.csv is available, append it only as supervised auxiliary training data with shared columns and locality weights based on median/MAD-standardized alpha, delta, u, g, r, i, z, and redshift nearest-neighbor distance to competition rows, clipped to a conservative range. Then fit a simple independent model panel, log per-model fold scores/runtimes, select the best single model for submission, and write submission.csv, oof_predictions.csv.gz, and test_predictions.csv.gz. Exclude variant weighting, affine calibration, class-wise blend tuning, stacking, temperature scaling, and hyperparameter search.
expected_effect: A successful run should show one simple model near the source single-expert range around 0.9675 balanced accuracy under 5-fold CV, with XGBoost or LightGBM likely strongest. If performance is much lower, the source run's advantage probably came from its ensemble/calibration rather than from the feature recipe alone.

source_cv_reference.note: Use the new materialized run's best simple model CV=5 score as the hypothesis score; do not reuse the source final blended score.

autogluon_feature_transfer.source: legacy feature engineering only; model, fold, calibration, and blending logic intentionally omitted
